en.text: O Droṇāchārya, best of the twice-born
O lord of the earth, Dhṛtarāṣṭra
O descendant of the Vṛṣṇi dynasty
when the family is overcome by irreligion
the performer of mighty tasks, Bhīmasen (sounded)
army of seven akṣauhiṇīs (one akṣauhiṇī is 21,870 chariots, 21,870 elephants, 65,610 horses, and 109,350 foot-soldiers)
at the holy land of Kurukṣetra
having seen the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra arrayed before him ready for battle
by Arjuna, the conqueror of sleep
of what value is our kingdom
and what is the need of its royal pleasures, or life itself
of the destroyers of the dynasty
the evil of destroying the dynasty?
With the decline of the dynasty
for both the dynasty and its destroyers
King Yudhiṣṭhir, the son of Kuntī
their hearts being afflicted with greed
due to the cessation of obsequial offerings of food and water
not to speak of ruling the earth?
and my mouth is becoming dry
nor the enjoyment of a kingdom
kingdom with its pleasures and enjoyable objects
Kṛpa, who is victorious in battle
at all points of entrance into the formation
when the women of the family become corrupt
the conchshells named Sughoṣa and Maṇipuṣpaka
for the sovereignty of even the three worlds
and prepared to sacrifice their lives
and the powerful King of Kāśī
Out of greed for royal pleasures

vocabulary.word: śaṅkhāḥ cha bheryaḥ cha paṇava-ānaka gomukhāḥ

en.text: conchshells, kettledrums, hand-drums, bugles, and other instruments

ru.text: во всех стратегических местах боевого построения
благодаря защите опытного Бхӣш̣мы (а также потому, что Кр̣ш̣н̣а отдал Свою армию Кауравам)
каким вопиющим преступлением является убийство собственных сородичей
рожки, маленькие барабаны, литавры и другие инструменты
обряды подношения освященной воды и пищи
и волосы на теле встают дыбом
и царь Ш́аибйа, благороднейший из людей
